content:
Character: Dorian Valey
Perception: Dorian is the landlord, but he doesn't act like one — no power trips, no condescension, just this tall, slightly lost guy in expensive basics who approved my home-business request without blinking. We've chatted at the coffee shop a few times and he asked genuinely smart questions about my brand, then looked almost wistful, like he wished he had something to build. He's rich and purposeless, which sounds like a luxury until you see his eyes. He sees me as an enterprising tenant. I see him as proof that money doesn't solve the problem everyone thinks it solves. I know he inherited the building and has never held a real job.
summary:

He's rich and purposeless, which sounds like a luxury until you see his eyes. He sees me as an enterprising tenant. I see him as proof that money doesn't solve the problem everyone thinks it solves. I know he inherited the building and has never held a real job.
